# How to Help Cannabis Plants in a Power Outage
by Nebula Haze
* * *
**Table of Contents**
[Emergency Ideas for a Power Outage](https://www.growweedeasy.com/electricity-power-outage#emergency-ideas-power-outage)
  * [Grow Lights Go Out](https://www.growweedeasy.com/electricity-power-outage#grow-lights)
  * [Temperature Drops](https://www.growweedeasy.com/electricity-power-outage#temperature)
  * [Hydroponic Air Pump Stops](https://www.growweedeasy.com/electricity-power-outage#dwc-hydro-growers)

[Backup Sources of Power](https://www.growweedeasy.com/electricity-power-outage#alternative-power-sources)
* * *
There are many things you can do to help your cannabis plants during a power outage even if you’re totally unprepared. There are also lots of ways to prepare so things go even more smoothly next time!
What can I do for my cannabis plants when the electricity goes out?
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/during-blackout-no-electricity.jpg)
**Emergency Ideas for a Power Outage** (and how to prepare for next time!)
Of course it’s best to have alternative sources of power, but here’s a list of things you can do right away during an electrical outage, even if you don’t have a backup generator or other source of electricity.  
**1.) What to Do When Grow Lights Go Out**
The most important thing when grow lights go out is to make sure your plant is getting some amount of light during its regular light period.
A plant left alone completely in the dark for more than a day starts deteriorating quickly, but you’ll slow this process down by giving them even low levels of light!
During your plant’s normal “Lights On” period, try to give at least a little light to your plant

Although it’s best to have a constant source of light, even periodically flashing a flashlight over the plant for a few minutes every hour or two can help plants maintain their regular light cycles!
Your plants will obviously do better with full lights on, but your main goal is to prevent them from being in the dark for long stretches of time during their “day” period. With all the other stress that may be happening, you want them to think it’s a cloudy day, not the end of the world!
A single small light during the “day” period is enough to maintain your plant’s inner clock

**Don’t forget to turn the light source _off_ before the plant’s regular “dark” period, especially in the flowering stage!** You most likely won’t have a timer so you must remember to shut lights off manually (or take the plant away from the window) before their dark period begins.
  * If your plant is in the _vegetative_ stage, it’s better to give extra hours of light as opposed to too little since your plant needs long days to stay in the vegetative stage.
  * If your plant is in the _flowering_ stage, it’s better to give extra hours of darkness if you have to choose between the two, since the plant needs short days to stay in the flowering stage.

For those wondering if vegetative plants will [start flowering](https://www.growweedeasy.com/light-schedules) after a day of darkness, don’t worry, one day isn’t going to cause it to start flowering! But when the power goes out you want to try to cover all your bases as best you can to minimize stress by maintaining constant light cycles. Why not help keep the plant as happy as possible? 🙂
**Prepare For a Future Emergency**
Get extra sources of battery-powered lights! This is a good idea even if you don’t have cannabis plants to care for. No one wants to be left in the dark when the power goes out!
As far as things to get ahead of time to prepare in case you need a little extra light in an emergency, some ways to provide light without electricity include…
  * [Strong Flashlight](https://www.amazon.com/CSNDICE-Rechargeable-Searchlight-High-power-flashlights/dp/B074P4ST39?keywords=flashlight+lumens+long&qid=1639188294&sr=8-6&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=5c570723f7c4b9ec426daee193b19517&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (either left on continuously or used in intervals for a few minutes every hour or two; this one lasts 24+ hours on a charge)
  * [Battery-Powered Lantern](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B01HMTYJJ4?&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=cd4085a515e86edb03d0ef46ca5a53a7&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
  * [Battery-Powered Christmas Lights](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B08HH2JZMS?psc=1&pd_rd_i=B08HH2JZMS&pd_rd_w=0KrT8&pf_rd_p=887084a2-5c34-4113-a4f8-b7947847c308&pd_rd_wg=Rlq48&pf_rd_r=YD08WR2989NDTN9K0ZHG&pd_rd_r=2def7818-294b-4fb7-ace2-5b40b0644c91&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUE0VE5HNDQ0NzlPTEwmZW5jcnlwdGVkSWQ9QTA3MjMzNDJVMFpTTUlJMzNDMTEmZW5jcnlwdGVkQWRJZD1BMDc4NTE4ODMyM0MwWUZCVFFQR08md2lkZ2V0TmFtZT1zcF9kZXRhaWwmYWN0aW9uPWNsaWNrUmVkaXJlY3QmZG9Ob3RMb2dDbGljaz10cnVl&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=64d088e1d42b7f3f2f4dfa46df6f752b&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
  * [Flameless Candle](https://www.amazon.com/GiveU-Flameless-Outdoor-Battery-Operated/dp/B00IMWIYGU?pd_rd_i=B00IMWIYGU&psc=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=1786598ff9e16ae6b1ccc9e56f81281e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
  * [CFL bulb](https://www.amazon.com/Light-Lumens-EcoSmart-Lights-GoodBulb/dp/B08QTVGZX8?keywords=40w+cfl&qid=1638249063&s=hi&sr=1-3-spons&psc=1&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUEzTjNFSlQxRDEzRzZQJmVuY3J5cHRlZElkPUExMDIzOTQ1M0hTNDdLU0lMNVRGTiZlbmNyeXB0ZWRBZElkPUEwMDcwNTM4R1lHS0RWUDZFVFVGJndpZGdldE5hbWU9c3BfYXRmJmFjdGlvbj1jbGlja1JlZGlyZWN0JmRvTm90TG9nQ2xpY2s9dHJ1ZQ%3D%3D&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=760509a2e53d0480f4537e4ed2bf0b5b&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) connected to a battery or other [power source](https://www.growweedeasy.com/electricity-power-outage#alternative-power-sources)

Have more than candles on hand in case the power goes out!
[](https://www.amazon.com/CSNDICE-Rechargeable-Searchlight-High-power-flashlights/dp/B074P4ST39?keywords=flashlight+lumens+long&qid=1639188294&sr=8-6&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=39312455553d8736b60b9c2624f1996e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)[](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B01HMTYJJ4/ref=as_li_ss_il?&linkCode=li2&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=8376a7dc6d33d1f58d532f04b669842d) [](https://www.amazon.com/Kohree-Battery-Seasonal-Decorative-Christmas/dp/B0169W7F5U/ref=as_li_ss_il?ie=UTF8&qid=1476324025&sr=8-1&keywords=battery+powered+light+timer&th=1&linkCode=li2&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=8daec1717def2df739d059e1b69740ea) 
**2.) Temperature Starts Dropping**
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/snowing-outside-in-the-city.jpg)
A common reason for the electricity to go out is winter weather, and this often comes along with freezing cold temperatures.
When your heat and grow lights go out, your cannabis plants may be exposed to cold temperatures. Each strain is different, but most cannabis plants start showing symptoms when the temperature reaches 50°F and lower, and freezing temperatures can be lethal!
**When you’ve lost power, the name of the game is heat conservation** since producing heat generally takes a lot of electricity. If possible, try to insulate the plant in some way while it’s still warm from the lights. Even throwing some blankets over the tent can help.
Do whatever you can to help keep plants warm!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/ice-storm-temperature-drops.jpg)
Make sure plants are not sitting directly on a cold floor (like concrete), because if you can keep the roots a little warmer the plant will be more resilient to the cold. Just like with the rest of the house, avoid opening and closing any doors to the outside so you let as little cold air in as possible. Try to limit peeking so you can so you don’t let out heat from the grow space.
Keep cannabis plants away from cold windows if the power goes out. They want warmth, just like you.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/12/cannabis-plant-looking-out-window-at-winter-snow.jpg)
**Prepare For a Future Emergency**
When it comes to helping plants stay warm, you generally have the same options you do as for humans. Unless you have a backup source of heat, there’s not a lot you can do other than to do the best you can and hope the plant comes out okay. You might be surprised at what conditions cannabis can come back from!
**3.) DWC / Hydro Growers – Air Pump Stops Working**
The main problem when the power goes out in a hydroponic / DWC setup is roots die from lack of oxygen.
You normally have air being bubbled through the tank but when the air stone shuts off, the root’s source of oxygen is gone!
The first step if the power goes out for more than an hour is to temporarily lower the water level to about half the normal level to help your plant roots get more oxygen with the top half of the roots.
If the plant is starting to droop after a few hours, drain all the water from the tank, then slowly pump it back in again. This gives your roots a huge dose of oxygen!
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/pump-water-out-then-back-in.jpg)
If the electricity stays off for more than a few hours, drain out all the water out and then put it back in, like doing a reservoir change without actually changing the water. I use a [battery-powered water transfer pump](https://www.amazon.com/Sierra-Tools-Battery-Operated-Liquid-Transfer/dp/B000HEBR3I/ref=as_li_ss_tl?ie=UTF8&qid=1525719973&sr=8-5&keywords=water+transfer+pump+battery&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=a79ace715ec76c7946c4f0d03260353b) (pictured above) but you should do whatever you normally do!
This will provide a lot of oxygen to your roots as the water is drained, as well as temporarily oxygenate the water as it gets pumped or poured back in.
Repeating this process every few hours while the electricity is out will help roots survive quite a bit longer!
If it’s getting really cold and you have access to a wood stove, you might consider heating up just a little of the water before you add it back, to help keep the water temperature above 55°F.
Avoid Letting Reservoir Water Get Really Cold!
You can use a wood stove or fireplace to heat up water if the temperature starts dropping dangerously low. Keep reservoir water above 55°F (13°C).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2016/10/blackout-fireplace.jpg)
**_Note:_ Never add hot water directly to tank; mix in with cold water before pumping back into tank!**
If the roots stay warm it will help the plants survive even if the air is cold. Although it’s important to provide oxygen, never, ever let any part of the roots dry out, as that will kill your plant a lot faster than no oxygen!
**Prepare For a Future Emergency**
If possible, try to invest ahead of time in a [battery-powered air pump](https://www.amazon.com/Marine-Metal-Aeratr-Bubble-1-5V/dp/B000EYULIS?dchild=1&keywords=marine+aerator+pump+battery+powered&qid=1599340320&sr=8-7&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=9b75d202f95250315d14bbf12795d49e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (originally meant for shipping fish) and a pack of [D batteries](https://amzn.to/2eaLDG0).
A [battery-powered air pump](https://www.amazon.com/Marine-Metal-Aeratr-Bubble-1-5V/dp/B000EYULIS?dchild=1&keywords=marine+aerator+pump+battery+powered&qid=1599340320&sr=8-7&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=9b75d202f95250315d14bbf12795d49e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (comes with tubing and air stone) oxygenates water for up to 24 hours with a pair of [D batteries](https://amzn.to/2eaLDG0)
[](https://www.amazon.com/Marine-Metal-Aeratr-Bubble-1-5V/dp/B000EYULIS?dchild=1&keywords=marine+aerator+pump+battery+powered&qid=1599340320&sr=8-7&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=f681b77419bf1ae17eb0177571f69958&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
[](https://www.amazon.com/AmazonBasics-Everyday-Alkaline-Batteries-12-Pack/dp/B00MH4QKP6/ref=as_li_ss_tl?ie=UTF8&qid=1476323367&sr=8-3&keywords=d+batteries&linkCode=sl1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=54fec98bf959923a452d1dc7e56e979b)
These battery-powered air pumps/stones provide your plant’s roots with air for up to 24 hours per battery charge, which means they’ll last as long as you have batteries for them. This way you don’t have to do manual reservoir changes every few hours to keep your plants alive.
Alternative Ways to Get Power
**Inverter**
Inverters that [plug into the cigarette lighter](https://www.amazon.com/BESTEK-Power-Inverter-Converter-Adapter/dp/B004MDXS0U?ie=UTF8&qid=1476322723&sr=8-1&keywords=inverter&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=35292539541239d9d5f158e5721c97d9&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) on your car can be a good backup source of energy, but you need to be able to reach your grow space from your car with an extension cord, and they only provide up to about 250-300W of power. If you have a garage or carport with direct access to your grow space, this could be a great choice to temporarily run pumps or a small backup light.
[](https://www.amazon.com/BESTEK-Power-Inverter-Converter-Adapter/dp/B004MDXS0U/ref=as_li_ss_il?ie=UTF8&qid=1476322723&sr=8-1&keywords=inverter&linkCode=li2&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=e5066ad730b51495cdd947638e51cd38)
**Uninterrupted Power Supply (UPS)**
An Uninterrupted Power Supply (UPS) is a great choice for protecting your computers during a power outage, but isn’t as great for your plants. A UPS is meant to give you enough time to safely turn off your computer/electronics after a blackout so they don’t lose power abruptly, but they’re incredibly expensive if you want one that supplies enough electricity to last a long time.
A UPS doesn’t last long or hold a big charge. They’re best for protecting equipment and computers, and aren’t that great as a backup power source unless you’re willing to spend a lot of money on a big one!
[](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B005GZRUZW/ref=as_li_ss_il?ie=UTF8&linkCode=li2&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=bbbb9c9179128013fbee6658f2efbab1)
**Gas-Powered Generator**
Gas-powered [generators](https://www.amazon.com/DuroStar-DS4000S-Starting-Portable-Generator/dp/B004918MO2?ie=UTF8&qid=1476323232&sr=8-3&keywords=generator&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=1a75f521ddcb9f673c882b7db0db893b&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) are another way to power things until the electricity comes back. If you live in an area where the power goes out a lot I could see how a generator could be almost necessary.
[](https://www.amazon.com/DuroStar-DS4000S-Starting-Portable-Generator/dp/B004918MO2/ref=as_li_ss_il?ie=UTF8&qid=1476323232&sr=8-3&keywords=generator&linkCode=li2&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=ed773a0f94c2a32a0c538b6e84ed8cae)
**Portable Power Supply + Bulb**
These are intended for recharging phones or laptops on-the-go or while camping, but portable power supplies are strong enough to run a small lightbulb for quite a few hours, so you’re not leaving your plants in the dark.
This [portable power supply](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07WQYLZFQ?&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=7857cc6f44025f6f8dc9e090385f840d&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) holds 250W. Plug a [9W LED bulb](https://www.amazon.com/Great-Eagle-Equivalent-Non-dimmable-Replacement/dp/B07QKGW18K?keywords=led+bulb&qid=1639189505&s=hi&sr=1-7&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=9f9f6a44c83b16d00dc240a693933cea&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) into the power supply with a [hanging light cord](https://www.amazon.com/2-Pack-Extension-Hanging-Pendant-Lantern/dp/B09FF774FX?keywords=light%2Bsocket&qid=1639189387&s=hi&sr=1-1-spons&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUExRTFOTDY1WlJPOVE3JmVuY3J5cHRlZElkPUEwNDg1NTMyMVJHTUw2SDZTNzY5RSZlbmNyeXB0ZWRBZElkPUExMDMyOTA5MzJISkRSQVVOSVdCWCZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2F0ZiZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=926098e2b07a769041dffd480bb0ed24&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (or other light fixture you have around the house) and get continuous light for over 24 hours. You can even plug a timer into it to maintain a 12/12 light schedule.
[Portable power supply](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07WQYLZFQ?&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=7857cc6f44025f6f8dc9e090385f840d&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) (250W)
[](https://www.amazon.com/dp/B07WQYLZFQ?&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=7857cc6f44025f6f8dc9e090385f840d&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
Plug in a light fixture or [hanging light cord](https://www.amazon.com/2-Pack-Extension-Hanging-Pendant-Lantern/dp/B09FF774FX?keywords=light%2Bsocket&qid=1639189387&s=hi&sr=1-1-spons&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUExRTFOTDY1WlJPOVE3JmVuY3J5cHRlZElkPUEwNDg1NTMyMVJHTUw2SDZTNzY5RSZlbmNyeXB0ZWRBZElkPUExMDMyOTA5MzJISkRSQVVOSVdCWCZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2F0ZiZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=926098e2b07a769041dffd480bb0ed24&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
[](https://www.amazon.com/2-Pack-Extension-Hanging-Pendant-Lantern/dp/B09FF774FX?keywords=light%2Bsocket&qid=1639189387&s=hi&sr=1-1-spons&spLa=ZW5jcnlwdGVkUXVhbGlmaWVyPUExRTFOTDY1WlJPOVE3JmVuY3J5cHRlZElkPUEwNDg1NTMyMVJHTUw2SDZTNzY5RSZlbmNyeXB0ZWRBZElkPUExMDMyOTA5MzJISkRSQVVOSVdCWCZ3aWRnZXROYW1lPXNwX2F0ZiZhY3Rpb249Y2xpY2tSZWRpcmVjdCZkb05vdExvZ0NsaWNrPXRydWU&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=926098e2b07a769041dffd480bb0ed24&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
Screw in an energy-efficient light like these [9w LED bulbs](https://www.amazon.com/Great-Eagle-Equivalent-Non-dimmable-Replacement/dp/B07QKGW18K?keywords=led+bulb&qid=1639189505&s=hi&sr=1-7&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=9f9f6a44c83b16d00dc240a693933cea&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
[](https://www.amazon.com/Great-Eagle-Equivalent-Non-dimmable-Replacement/dp/B07QKGW18K?keywords=led+bulb&qid=1639189505&s=hi&sr=1-7&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=9f9f6a44c83b16d00dc240a693933cea&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**Don’t give up until your plant is dead. Speaking from experience, you might be surprised at what they can survive.**
* * *
Learn how to prepare your grow room for [other kinds of emergencies!](https://www.growweedeasy.com/common-growing-emergencies)

GrowWeedEasy.com was [founded in 2010](https://www.growweedeasy.com/about) by Nebula Haze and Sirius Fourside. Featured in _High Times_ , Ed Rosenthal’s books, _Cannabis Business Times_ , and _Skunk Magazine_ , GrowWeedEasy.com is the **#1 free resource in the world** for learning how to grow cannabis at home. Our simple step-by-step tutorials make it easy for anyone to grow great weed. **[Sign up for our free newsletter](https://www.growweedeasy.com/message/news)** to get grow tips delivered every Sunday at 4:20 AM.
